summary: Bridge-free trend comparison across six det=1.25 MHz running-autosave snapshots (2, 4, 6, 8, 10, and 14 balanced averages). Carrier and det+13C target-region amplitudes remain about percent scale but softened at 14 averages (carrier 0.94%, det+13C 1.02%), while old det=1 high/static and previous 1.9 MHz regions remain weak (0.28% and 0.23%). This still favors terminal focus on the det=1.25 carrier/high-sideband model over static old-high/1.9-MHz alternatives, but remains running-autosave provenance only; terminal 16-average review is required before any T2star or 13C conclusion.
